perturbation.original_text: The party receiving Confidential Information (the “Receiving Party”) shall no t disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the party disclosing such confidential information (the “Disclosing Party”) and shallrestrict dissemination of Confidential Information within its own organization to those employees who have a need to have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The party receiving Protected Data (the “Receiving Party”) shall no t disclose Protected Data to any third party without the prior written consent of the party disclosing such confidential information (the “Disclosing Party”) and shallrestrict dissemination of Protected Data within its own organization to those employees who have a need to have access to the Protected Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' without defining 'Protected Data'. The term "Confidential Information" is clearly defined, whereas the term 'Protected Data' is not. This creates uncertainty as the original text defines "Confidential Information", while "Protected Data" remains undefined. Later clauses reference only "Confidential Information," now making it unclear which information is actually protected under the contract. This change affects the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party , the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information; (ii) promptly return the Confidential Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the Disclosing Party within seven (7)da ys of receipt of demand; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Confidential Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data,' which is not defined. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information.' Because the changed text introduces an undefined term and the contradicted text only references the defined 'Confidential Information,' there is now an inconsistency as to what information the Receiving Party is obligated to return or destroy. This creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party , the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information; (ii) promptly return the Confidential Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the Disclosing Party within seven (7)da ys of receipt of demand; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Confidential Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon written demand by the Disclosing Party , the Receiving Party shall: (i) cease using the Proprietary Information; (ii) promptly return the Proprietary Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the Disclosing Party within seven (7)da ys of receipt of demand; and/or (iii) upon request of the Disclosing Party, destroy all such Proprietary Information, including copies thereof, and shall furnish the Disclosing Party with written certification of destruction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Information" in the section regarding the Return or Destruction of Confidential Information introduces a contradiction. The term "Proprietary Information" is not previously defined in the agreement. By creating a new term and not explaining it, the contradiction makes it unclear what must be returned. It creates a contradiction, affecting enforcement of the return obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Confidential Information" as used herein means all trade secrets or confidential or proprietary information of either party, whether or not marked “Confidential”, whether in written ororal form, rel ating to such party's business or technology, including without limitation, its products, customer data, development and marketing plans, and financial information. In addition, Confidential Information shall include all terms and conditions of this Agreement including the fact that the parties are having discussions in order to determine the feasibility of a commercial relationship.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information' in a section detailing the return or destruction obligations. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information', but 'Proprietary Information' is not defined anywhere in the document. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear if the obligations in the changed text align with what is considered 'Confidential Information' as defined in the contradicted text, making the enforcement of the return/destruction obligation ambiguous.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall retain all right, title and interest to such party’s Confidential Information disclosed to the other party. Subject only to the Receiving Party’s limited use of the Confidential I nformation for the purpose set forth above, the Receiving Party acknowledges a nd ag rees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or implying any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement whether under any trademark, patent or copyright, or application of same which are now or thereafter may be obtained by such party. The Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party’s intellectual property orother rights in or to the Confidential Information. AL L CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY R EPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. I N NO EVENT SHALL THE DISCLOSING PARTY BE LIABLE FOR THE ACCURACY OR COMPLETENESS OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall retain all right, title and interest to such party’s Sensitive Data disclosed to the other party. Subject only to the Receiving Party’s limited use of the Confidential I nformation for the purpose set forth above, the Receiving Party acknowledges a nd ag rees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or implying any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement whether under any trademark, patent or copyright, or application of same which are now or thereafter may be obtained by such party. The Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party’s intellectual property orother rights in or to the Confidential Information. AL L CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY R EPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. I N NO EVENT SHALL THE DISCLOSING PARTY BE LIABLE FOR THE ACCURACY OR COMPLETENESS OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 6, changing “Confidential Information” to “Sensitive Data” in the first sentence, while maintaining references to "Confidential Information" in the rest of the paragraph, creates an internal contradiction. It's unclear whether the rights and limitations described apply to both "Sensitive Data" and "Confidential Information," or only to the latter. This inserted perturbation causes uncertainty, especially in determining ownership and usage rights. If one party discloses "Sensitive Data," but the limitations only apply to "Confidential Information," the disclosing party's rights are significantly weakened.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall retain all right, title and interest to such party’s Sensitive Data disclosed to the other party. Subject only to the Receiving Party’s limited use of the Confidential I nformation for the purpose set forth above, the Receiving Party acknowledges a nd ag rees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or implying any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement whether under any trademark, patent or copyright, or application of same which are now or thereafter may be obtained by such party. The Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party’s intellectual property orother rights in or to the Confidential Information. AL L CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROVIDED “AS IS” WITHOUT ANY R EPRESENTATIONS OR WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. I N NO EVENT SHALL THE DISCLOSING PARTY BE LIABLE FOR THE ACCURACY OR COMPLETENESS OF ANY CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces "Confidential Information" with "Sensitive Data" in the first sentence of the paragraph, while the rest of the paragraph continues to refer to "Confidential Information." This misalignment of terminology creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the subsequent clauses related to rights, licenses, intellectual property, warranties, and liability apply to Sensitive Data, Confidential Information, or both. Since the contradicted text is the changed text, there is a clear and direct conflict, thus satisfying the conditions for a "YES" response.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Understanding shall remain in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Effective Date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the word "Agreement" to "Understanding" in section 7 creates a potential ambiguity. The initial definition refers to a "MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT”, then section 7 refers to “Understanding.” It creates doubt as to whether the whole document, and all it's terms, is still valid, affecting the validity of all related clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“Agreement”) made this _____ day of _____ 20___, between YOUR COMPANY having a principal place of business at YOUR COMPANY ADDRESS and ____________________,whose principal address is ___________________________________________________.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Agreement" to "Understanding" creates a contradiction because the contradicted text specifically defines the document as a "MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“Agreement”)." By changing the term to "Understanding" in the modified text, it introduces ambiguity and calls into question whether the provisions of the document still apply, thus creating a strong contradiction. This aligns with the definition of "Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction" where the interchangeable use of terms without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof, and is the final, complete and exclusive expression of t he terms and conditions thereof. All prior or contemporaneous agreements, representations, negotiations and understandings of the parties hereto, oral or written, express or implied, are hereby superseded and merged herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Document constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto pertaining to the subject matter hereof, and is the final, complete and exclusive expression of t he terms and conditions thereof. All prior or contemporaneous agreements, representations, negotiations and understandings of the parties hereto, oral or written, express or implied, are hereby superseded and merged herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Agreement" to "Document" introduces a contradiction. The initial definition refers to a "MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT.” This change in terminology creates uncertainty about what the 'entire agreement' actually encompasses. If 'Document' refers only to the physical pages, it might exclude referenced materials or prior communications that were intended to be included in the 'Agreement'. This could weaken the enforceability of the contract and allow parties to argue that certain aspects aren't covered by the non-disclosure obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (“Agreement”) made this _____ day of _____ 20___, between YOUR COMPANY having a principal place of business at YOUR COMPANY ADDRESS and ____________________,whose principal address is ___________________________________________________.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to an 'Agreement,' which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' as a 'MUTUAL NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'. The changed text replaces 'Agreement' with 'Document.' This creates a contradiction because 'Document' is not explicitly defined, leading to ambiguity about what constitutes the 'entire agreement'. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where the change in terminology creates uncertainty about what the 'entire agreement' encompasses, potentially excluding referenced materials that were intended to be included in the 'Agreement'.
